name: Subcarpathian Voivodeship (Poland)

colors.symbolism: the field of the regional coat of arms

description: Light-blue field charged at centre with the quartered coat of arms of the Subcarpathian Voivodeship on a white panel.
history: Adopted by the Subcarpathian Voivodeship after its creation in 1999, drawing on the heraldry of the historic territories that now make up the region.

features.symbolism: the field colour of the regional coat of arms

features.role: arms of the Subcarpathian Voivodeship — a quartered shield combining the lion of the Ruthenian land, the eagle of Lesser Poland, and the cross of Sandomierz
features.symbolism: represents the historic territories united in the present voivodeship
